data.narrative:
Asylon Robotics is a Series B–stage drone and autonomous systems company that has raised $40M to date. The broader Drones & Autonomous sector comprises eight private peers including Elroy Air, Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, and Archer Aviation, with no public comparables available for direct benchmarking.

Given the nascent maturity of the sector and limited same-stage peer data with sufficient granularity, peer-median valuation was not applicable. A single methodological approach—stage-progression analysis—was instead employed. Using the $40M total raised as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-round progression, this method yields an implied valuation of $76M.

The stage-progression framework suggests a valuation range of $65M to $87M, with a median point estimate of $76M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the median. The primary uncertainty stems from reliance on a single triangulation method; validation against peer revenue multiples or customer metrics would strengthen conviction but was constrained by limited publicly disclosed financials among Drones & Autonomous companies. Investors should regard this estimate as provisional pending access to Asylon's unit economics and market positioning relative to its peer cohort.
